name: Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA)
summary: The Foreign Corrupt Practices Act prohibits US persons and entities from bribing foreign government officials to obtain or retain business. It also requires SEC-registered companies to maintain accurate books and records and adequate internal accounting controls, making it a cornerstone of international anti-corruption enforcement.
keyRequirements: Anti-bribery provisions: prohibit payments to foreign officials to influence acts or secure improper advantage
Books and records provisions: maintain accurate books, records, and accounts in reasonable detail
Internal controls: devise and maintain adequate internal accounting controls
Third-party due diligence for agents, consultants, and business partners
Applies to US issuers, domestic concerns, and foreign nationals acting within US territory
No exception for facilitating or grease payments under DOJ enforcement policy
penalties: Criminal penalties: up to $250,000 and 5 years imprisonment for individuals; up to $2 million per violation for entities. Civil penalties up to $16,000 per violation. Disgorgement of profits, pre-judgment interest, and injunctive relief. DOJ and SEC concurrent enforcement authority.
